content:
First write \(r=[r]+ \{r\}=n+f\) with integer \(n\ge0\) and \(0\le f<1\).  The equation is

\[
25\,f + n = 125
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
f=\frac{125-n}{25}=5-\frac{n}{25}.
\]

Since \(0\le f<1\) we need
\[
0\le 5-\frac n{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
0\le5-\frac n{25}
\quad\text{and}\quad
5-\frac n{25}<1,
\]
i.e.
\[
n\le125
\quad\text{and}\quad
n>100.
\]
Hence \(n\in\{101,102,\dots,125\}\), and for each such \(n\)
\[
r=n+f
=n+\Bigl(5-\frac n{25}\Bigr)
=5+\frac{24\,n}{25}.
\]
We sum over \(n=101\) to \(125\).  There are 25 terms, so
\[
\sum r
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}\!\Bigl(5+\frac{24n}{25}\Bigr)
=25\cdot5+\frac{24}{25}\sum_{n=101}^{125}n
=125+\frac{24}{25}\cdot\bigl(113\cdot25\bigr)
=125+24\cdot113
=2837.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{2837}\).
